courses.name: Neuroscience of Mental Health and Psychiatric Conditions
courses.description: An overview of mental health conditions, exploring biological, developmental, experiential and environmental factors that contribute to their onset and expression. Topics may include depressive and anxiety disorders, schizophrenia, autism, ADHD, eating disorders, sleep-related conditions, and substance use disorders.

courses.name: Neuroscience of Mental Health and Neurological Conditions
courses.description: An overview of neurological conditions, examining biological, developmental, experiential and environmental contributions. Topics may include stroke, multiple sclerosis, seizure conditions, Parkinson’s disease, ALS, chronic pain, Alzheimer’s disease and concussion.

courses.name: Introduction to Research Methods in Neuroscience
courses.description: A general introduction to research process within neuroscience. Topics covered include research strategies, methods, and techniques; basic descriptive statistics; research communication; and responsible scientific conduct.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: Introduction to Statistics in Neuroscience
courses.description: A general introduction to statistical techniques employed within contemporary neuroscience. Topics covered include basic data analysis using descriptive and inferential statistics (t-tests, ANOVA, correlation, chi-square).
courses.prerequisites: PSYC 2001 or NEUR 2001 .

courses.name: Introduction to Techniques in Neuroscience
courses.description: Introduction to common techniques used in neuroscience research. Brain imaging, animal behaviour, electrophysiology, immunohistochemistry and microscopy, genomics, transgenics, cell culture, and DSM-IV-based clinical assessment.
courses.prerequisites: one of PSYC 1001 , NEUR 1201, NEUR 1202 or NEUR 1203 .

courses.name: Fundamentals of Scientific Writing in Neuroscience
courses.description: Introduction to various forms of scientific writing appropriate to neuroscience, with a focus in fundamental skills in scientific writing.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing in a Neuroscience program and one of NEUR 1201, NEUR 1202 or NEUR 1203 .

courses.name: Cellular and Molecular Neuroscience
courses.description: Core principles in cellular and molecular neuroscience, including signal transmission along and between neurons, ion channels and transporters, intracellular signaling pathways, and regulation of gene expression.
courses.prerequisites: Either NEUR 1201 and NEUR 1203 , or NEUR 1202 and NEUR 1203 , or both BIOL 1103 and BIOL 1104 .

courses.name: Neurodevelopment and Plasticity
courses.description: Core principles in nervous system development from embryogenesis to plasticity in the adult brain. Topics include neural induction, neurogenesis, apoptosis, neuronal migration and axon growth, synaptogenesis and synaptic pruning both under normal conditions and in psychopathology.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 1202 and NEUR 1203 .

courses.name: Neuroscience and Creativity
courses.description: Altered brain function related to mental health or substance use has often inspired cultural works including films, music, visual art and literature. This course explores the neurobiological basis of creativity in individuals with and without mental health conditions.
courses.prerequisites: one of PSYC 1001 , NEUR 1201, NEUR 1202 or NEUR 1203 .

courses.name: Data Analysis in Neuroscience I
courses.description: Introducing various software for analyzing neuroscience data. Dealing with real data, drawing graphs, application of descriptive and inferential statistics through the general linear model, assumptions of parametric tests, robust statistics, confidence intervals, correlations, use of appropriate statistical methods and interpretation of results.
courses.prerequisites: PSYC 2001 and PSYC 2002 , or NEUR 2001 and NEUR 2002 .

courses.name: Data Analysis in Neuroscience II
courses.description: Use of software for analyzing neuroscience data. Statistical techniques typically include nonparametric tests, t tests, and various forms of both ANOVA and regression including robust statistical tests, with a focus on the practical application of appropriate statistical methods and interpretation of results.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 3001 .

courses.name: Epidemiology in Neuroscience
courses.description: Introduction to the principles and methods of epidemiology, study designs, measures of effect, sources of error, confounding, bias, internal and external validity, and causality. The course also will provide an overview of the epidemiological features, and risk factors for common neurological disorders.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 2002 .

courses.name: Field Course in Animal Behaviour
courses.description: Offered in the Department of Biology as . Only those modules dealing with animal behaviour topics may be offered for Neuroscience credit.
courses.prerequisites: permission of the department.

courses.name: Neuropharmacology
courses.description: Overview of chemical neurotransmission and key neurotransmitter systems.  A description of licit and illicit drugs covering topics that range from historical perspectives to pharmacology to mechanisms of action in the brain.  Discussion of neurochemical basis of psychiatric diseases including anxiety, depression and schizophrenia.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 2200 or NEUR 2201 .

courses.name: Sensory and Motor Neuroscience
courses.description: Exploration of major topics in sensory processing and motor control, with a focus on underlying mechanisms and neurobiological principles. Topics include all sensory systems (such as vision, somatosensation and audition) plus motor system components including lower and upper motor neurons, basal ganglia, and cerebellum.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 1201 or both NEUR 1202 and NEUR 1203 , and either NEUR 2200 or both NEUR 2201 and NEUR 2202 .

courses.name: Systems Neuroscience
courses.description: Neural systems underlying complex behaviours including emotion, motivation, and sleep, and the role of association cortices in brain function.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 3206 .

courses.name: Genetics of Mental Health
courses.description: Many common mental health conditions have a genetic component. This course explores how genetic susceptibility is identified and examines the genetic, genomic and epigenetic mechanisms that may influence vulnerability to these conditions.
courses.prerequisites: BIOL 2104 or BIOL 2107 , and NEUR 2200 or NEUR 2201 .

courses.name: The Neuroscience of Consciousness
courses.description: Consciousness remains one of the least understood aspects of the nervous system. This course explores neural mechanisms underlying consciousness, changes in consciousness associated with sleep, coma, vegetative states, drugs, and other stimuli, and considers the evolutionary basis of consciousness, and its relationship with awareness.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 2200 or NEUR 2202 .

courses.name: Hormones and Behaviour
courses.description: The effects of hormones throughout life at all levels of the nervous system. The role of hormones in mediating behaviours that are both basic (feeding, reproduction and social interactions) and complex (motivation, emotion, learning and memory).
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 2200 or both NEUR 2201 and NEUR 2202 .

courses.name: Environmental Toxins and Mental Health
courses.description: Exposure to environmental toxins from the air, water or food can interfere with neuronal function, alter neurodevelopment, and damage the brain. This course will explore associations between toxins and diseases such as Parkinson’s disease, multiple sclerosis and depression, focusing on mechanisms underlying development of pathology.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 2200 or both NEUR 2201 and NEUR 2202 .

courses.name: Impact of Lifestyle and Social Interactions on Mental Health
courses.description: Healthy lifestyle choices and positive social interactions can reduce the incidence of pathological conditions such as depression, obesity, cardiovascular disease and impaired immunity. This course focuses on psychosocial and neurobiological mechanisms that underlie the relationship between lifestyle, social interactions and health.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 2200 or both NEUR 2201 and NEUR 2202 .

courses.name: Stress and Mental Health
courses.description: Stressful events can have profound repercussions on physical and psychological well-being. This course examines the psychosocial and biological processes by which stressors predispose to both physical (immune-related disorders, diabetes, heart disease) and psychological (acute stress disorder, posttraumatic stress disorder, depression, anxiety) pathologies.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 2200 or both NEUR 2201 and NEUR 2202 .

courses.name: Neurodegeneration and Aging
courses.description: Perspectives on aging and neurodegeneration from psychosocial and neuroscience points of view.  How factors including TBI, stroke and alcohol make the brain vulnerable and contribute to neurodegeneration.  Clinical overview of Alzheimer’s, Parkinson’s, Huntington’s and ALS and the underlying pathology that differentiates these diseases.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 2200 or both NEUR 2201 and NEUR 2202 .

courses.name: Neurodevelopmental Determinants of Mental Health
courses.description: Exploration of human brain development, from cell differentiation to complex neural networks. This course considers how variations in neurodevelopment may be associated with conditions such as dyslexia, ADHD, schizophrenia and autism.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 2200, or both NEUR 2201 and NEUR 2202 .

courses.name: Special Topics in Neuroscience
courses.description: Each section of deals with a different topic. Topics change yearly. Students may register in more than one section of but can register in each section only once.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 3200, or NEUR 3204 and NEUR 3206 and NEUR 3207 , or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses
courses.description: Introduction to the methods used in conducting systematic reviews and meta-analyses. Topics include: conducting literature searches, extracting relevant literature, assessing quality of studies, synthesizing findings across studies, and the statistical methods used to carry out a meta-analysis.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 3003 or both NEUR 3001 and NEUR 3002 .

courses.name: Knowledge Mobilization
courses.description: Knowledge mobilization concepts, tools, and frameworks, the challenges and value of translational research, and processes involved in integrated knowledge mobilization. Skills to maximize research impacts will be developed.
courses.prerequisites: fourth year standing in a Neuroscience program OR permission of the department.

courses.name: Seminar on Current Advances in Neuroscience
courses.description: Headline research in neuroscience. Topics may include technical and conceptual advances, ethical issues, medical improvement, and social impacts of neuroscience research.
courses.prerequisites: fourth year standing and one of NEUR 3200, NEUR 3206 or NEUR 3207 .

courses.name: Seminar on Current Research in Neuroscience and Psychiatric Conditions
courses.description: Recent research in clinical neuroscience including biological, developmental, experiential and environmental factors that contribute to mental health conditions. Topics may include depression, schizophrenia, autism, ADHD, eating disorders, sleep-related conditions, substance use disorders, and personality-related conditions.
courses.prerequisites: fourth year standing and one of NEUR 3200, NEUR 3206 or NEUR 3207 .

courses.name: Seminar on Current Research in Neuroscience and Clinical Neurology
courses.description: Recent research in neurological disease, including biological, developmental, experiential and environmental factors that contribute to disease. Topics may include stroke, multiple sclerosis, migraine, seizure disorder, Parkinson’s disease, ALS, chronic pain, Alzheimer’s disease and concussion.
courses.prerequisites: fourth year standing and one of NEUR 3200, NEUR 3206 or NEUR 3207 .

courses.name: Neurobiology of Energy Homeostasis
courses.description: Focus on neuroanatomical and molecular mechanisms underlying how mammals adapt to changes and challenges in the environment. Topics include regulation of feeding, energy expenditure, water balance, and temperature regulation.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 3304 .

courses.name: Sex and the Brain
courses.description: Neurobiological processes behind reproductive behaviours in various animal species including humans. Evaluation of data concerning neurobiological differences between sexes, biological determinants of sexual orientation, and relating to neurobiology of sex disorders.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 3304 .

courses.name: Indigenous Health & Mental Health
courses.description: Explores physical and mental health issues in Indigenous communities through cultural, environmental, developmental and biological perspectives, with attention to the impacts of colonialism, intergenerational trauma, and community resilience.
courses.prerequisites: Third year standing or above.

courses.name: Immune-Brain Interactions
courses.description: Communication between the brain and the immune system; messengers mediating the interaction. How disturbances of immune-brain signaling can lead to disease (multiple sclerosis, Parkinson’s) and to changes in mood and cognition.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 3200 or NEUR 3207 .

courses.name: The Neural Basis of Addiction
courses.description: Explores how substance use and behavioural addictions impact neural function, contributing to changes in the brain system involved in addiction. Contemporary neurobiological theories of addiction will also be addressed.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 3204 .

courses.name: Advanced Lab in Neuroanatomy
courses.description: Advanced experiential learning in neuroanatomy, histology and microscopy.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 3200 or both NEUR 3206 and NEUR 3207 , fourth-year standing in a Neuroscience program, a minimum Major CGPA of 9.0 and permission of the Department.

courses.name: Neuroethics
courses.description: Ethical issues of key importance to current neurobiological research. Topics may include the use of animals in research, stem cell research, genetic diagnosis and gene therapy, neuroimaging, and the effect on identity and autonomy of manipulations such as psychopharmaceuticals and psychosurgery.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 3200 or both NEUR 3206 and NEUR 3207 .

courses.name: Independent Study
courses.description: A reading or research course for selected students who wish to investigate a particular topic of interest. Normally students may not offer more than one credit of independent study in their total program.
courses.prerequisites: third- or fourth- year standing and permission of the Department.

courses.name: Honours Research Thesis in Systematic Reviews or Meta-Analyses
courses.description: An independent systematic review or meta-analyses undertaken under the direct supervision of a faculty advisor typically from the Department of Neuroscience.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 3003 or both NEUR 3001 and NEUR 3002 and fourth-year standing in an Honours Neuroscience program, a minimum Major CGPA of 9.0 and permission of the Department.

courses.name: Honours Workshop
courses.description: The course will focus on active learning in areas that include written and oral communication, evaluation and interpretation of results, statistics and data management, emphasizing transferable skills that will be most appropriate for non-research career paths.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in an Honours Neuroscience program and permission of the Department.

courses.name: Translational Approach to Indigenous Community Wellness
courses.description: This course involves co-developing an Indigenous community-led process or product that addresses a current and specific mental health issue. Involves working in interdisciplinary groups with a community partner.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year standing with a minimum Major CGPA of 10.0 and a grade of A- or higher in one of NEUR 3401 , NEUR 3402 or NEUR 3403 and permission of instructor. Prior completion of NEUR 4303 recommended.

courses.name: Honours Essay
courses.description: An independent essay based critical review on a topic in neuroscience, using library resources, under the direct supervision of a Faculty advisor, typically from the Department of Neuroscience. Evaluation is based on a written report.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 3200, or both NEUR 3206 and NEUR 3207 , and fourth-year standing in an Honours Neuroscience program, a minimum Major CGPA of 9.0 and permission of the Department.

courses.name: Honours Research Thesis
courses.description: An independent research project undertaken under the direct supervision of a faculty advisor typically from the Department of Neuroscience. Evaluation is based on a written report and poster.
courses.prerequisites: NEUR 3200, or both NEUR 3206 and NEUR 3207 , and fourth-year standing in an Honours Neuroscience program, a minimum Major CGPA of 10.0 and permission of the Department.

free_electives.degree: Neuroscience and Mental Health B.Sc. Honours (20.0 credits)
Neuroscience and Mental Health B.Sc. Major (20.0 credits)
Neuroscience and Mental Health B.Sc. (15.0 credits)

free_electives.text: BIOL (Biology) except BIOL 4810 which may be used only as a free elective for any B.Sc. program. Biochemistry students may use BIOL 2005 only as a free elective.

ERTH (Earth Sciences), except ERTH 2415 which may be used only as a free elective for any B.Sc. program. Students in Earth Sciences programs may use ERTH 2401 , ERTH 2402 , and ERTH 2403 only as free electives.

TSES (Technology, Society, Environment) except TSES 2305 . Biology students may use these courses only as free electives. Integrated Science and Environmental Science students may include these courses in their programs but may not count them as part of the Science Sequence.
BIOC (Biochemistry) except BIOC 1900 which may be used only as a free elective for any B.Sc. program.
BIOL (Biology) except BIOL 4810 which may be used only as a free elective for any B.Sc. program. Biology & Biochemistry students may use BIOL 1010 and BIOL 2005 only as free electives.
ERTH (Earth Sciences) except ERTH 1004 and ERTH 2415 . Earth Sciences students may use ERTH 2401 , ERTH 2402 and ERTH 2403 only as free electives.
TSES (Technology, Society, Environment)  Biology students may use these courses only as free electives.

Any course is allowable as a Free Elective providing it is not prohibited (see below). Students are expected to comply with prerequisite requirements and enrolment restrictions for all courses as published in this Calendar.
Courses Allowable Only as Free Electives in any B.Sc. Program
